answer:
We need to balance the nuclear reactions by conserving mass number and atomic number.  

For (a): \( ^1\mathrm{H} + ^9\mathrm{Be} \rightarrow \mathrm{X} + n \) gives mass number \(1+9-1=9\) and atomic number \(1+4-0=5\), so X is \( ^9\mathrm{B} \).  

For (b): \( ^{12}\mathrm{C} + ^1\mathrm{H} \rightarrow \mathrm{X} \) gives mass number \(12+1=13\) and atomic number \(6+1=7\), so X is \( ^{13}\mathrm{N} \) (not in options, but (c) will match).  

For (c): \( ^{15}\mathrm{N} + ^1\mathrm{H} \rightarrow ^4\mathrm{He} + \mathrm{X} \) gives mass number \(15+1-4=12\) and atomic number \(7+1-2=6
